output.summary: Revision 2: Fixed redundant class_name() wrapper in theme_block_props() call. The theme_block_props() helper already calls class_name() internally (line 74 of inc/helpers/blocks.php), so wrapping the classes array in class_name() before passing it was redundant and unnecessary.

changes: Lines 41-51: Removed class_name() wrapper from theme_block_props() call
Now passes conditional array directly: ['featured-post py-10 sm:py-16' => true, 'dark bg-transparent!' => $darkMode]
filesModified.reasoning: The theme_block_props() helper function (inc/helpers/blocks.php line 74) already calls class_name() internally on the $classes parameter. Wrapping the array in class_name() before passing it causes a double-call where the output string of the first class_name() call is passed as input to the second class_name() call, which is redundant and incorrect pattern.

buildStatus.details: Build completed successfully with no TypeScript errors

helperFunctionAnalysis.behavior: Accepts a conditional array (e.g., ['class-name' => true, 'another-class' => false]) and internally calls class_name() to convert it to a space-separated string. Also handles string input by normalizing to array.
helperFunctionAnalysis.correctUsage: Pass conditional array directly: theme_block_props(['class' => true, 'other' => $condition])
helperFunctionAnalysis.incorrectUsage: Wrapping in class_name() first: theme_block_props(class_name(['class' => true])) - this is redundant

fixesApplied.description: Removed redundant class_name() wrapper in theme_block_props() call
fixesApplied.verification: theme_block_props() now receives conditional array directly. No class_name() wrapper on lines 43-48.

notes: This is a code quality improvement with no functional changes
The output HTML/CSS classes remain identical - only the internal processing changed
theme_block_props() is designed to accept conditional arrays and handle class_name() conversion internally
Frontend rendering verified at test page: https://ign.localhost/test-featured-post/

verifications.details: theme_block_props() now receives array directly on lines 44-47 without class_name() wrapper
bun run build completed successfully with only expected asset size warnings
Verified theme_block_props() accepts arrays and calls class_name() internally (inc/helpers/blocks.php line 74)
Output HTML remains identical - only internal processing simplified

before.issue: Double class_name() call - manual wrapper + internal helper call

after.improvement: Single class_name() call - only internal helper call (correct pattern)
